features.label: libwebrtc integration

patches.author: Nico Grunbaum
patches.subject: Bug 2047265 (MOZ) - switch from deprecated OnDroppedFrame to OnFrameDropped

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Allocate WebrtcVideoEncoderProxy with the MakeRefPtr utility function instead of 'new'.

patches.subject: Change MediaDataCodec::CreateDecoder() to return std::unique_ptr.

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Change MediaDataCodec::CreateEncoder() to return std::unique_ptr.

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Change GmpVideoCodec::CreateDecoder() to return std::unique_ptr.

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Change GmpVideoCodec::CreateEncoder() to return std::unique_ptr.

features.summary: Recent updates in Firefox’s integration with **libwebrtc**, the technology that underpins real-time communication features like video calls, include several important improvements to the way video data is managed within the browser. The switch from the deprecated OnDroppedFrame to OnFrameDropped enhances the handling of dropped video frames, ensuring smoother playback in video calls. Additionally, coding practices were refined by adopting _smart pointers_ like `std::unique_ptr` for better memory management when creating decoders and encoders for video data. These changes mean a more efficient and reliable experience for users engaging in video conferencing, making it crucial for keeping Firefox competitive in the realm of online communication.

features.label: Negotiation engine (JSEP)

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Use MakeUnique (instead of foo.reset(new T()) to repopulate a UniquePtr in JsepCodecDescription.h.

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Migrate JsepCodecDescription::Clone() to return UniquePtr.

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Migrate JsepSession::Clone() to return UniquePtr.

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Migrate various WebRTC 'Parameters* Clone()' methods to return UniquePtr.

features.summary: Recent changes in the negotiation engine, particularly in how WebRTC (the technology behind video calls) manages its internal objects, aim to improve memory management and efficiency. By transitioning functions like JsepCodecDescription::Clone() and JsepSession::Clone() to return UniquePtr, Firefox is enhancing the way it handles dynamic memory. This means less risk of memory leaks and better performance when managing WebRTC codec information and session data. **This update is crucial because it helps ensure a smoother, more reliable video calling experience for users** while also making the codebase safer and more maintainable.

features.label: WebRTC

patches.author: Philipp Hancke
patches.subject: add IPC serialization for keyFramesEncoded stats

features.summary: Recent updates to WebRTC, the technology that enables real-time communication like video calls, have introduced important enhancements to how performance statistics are tracked and reported. By adding IPC (Inter-Process Communication) serialization for keyFramesEncoded stats and implementing RTCTransportStats, developers can now better monitor the efficiency of data transmission, including the bytes and packets sent and received. This improved insight into network performance is crucial for optimizing user experiences during video calls. Additionally, code improvements related to memory management, particularly through the use of MakeRefPtr for device enumeration and source creation, aim to enhance reliability and resource handling within the audio and video input systems. Overall, these changes contribute to a more robust and accountable WebRTC framework for developers and users alike, ensuring smoother communication experiences.

features.label: WebRTC web API (RTCPeerConnection)

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC web API, which enables real-time communication like video calls, have focused on improving its performance and capabilities. Notably, a new feature has been added to track **key frames in video streams**, which will enhance the way video quality is monitored during calls by providing important statistics about keyFramesEncoded. Additionally, updates to the RTCRtcpParameters will improve how session control information is described, specifically the **cname** (Canonical Name) and reducedSize fields, which help ensure smoother connectivity and management of media streams. These enhancements collectively contribute to making real-time communication more efficient and reliable for users._

features.label: Connection transport (ICE / DTLS)

patches.author: Anna Weine
patches.subject: Implement RTCCertificateStats && RTCTransport certificate ids

patches.author: Philipp Hancke
patches.subject: implement candidate-pair packetsSent/packetsReceived

features.summary: Recent updates in the connection transport area of Firefox enhance the handling of WebRTC (the technology behind video calls). The implementation of **RTCCertificateStats** allows developers to access detailed statistics about the security certificates used during these communications, which can improve connection security and performance. Additionally, the inclusion of data regarding packet transmission—specifically, the number of packets sent and received in candidate pairs—provides crucial insights for optimizing connection quality and stability. These changes matter because they contribute to more secure and reliable video calling experiences for users.

features.label: Camera & audio capture

patches.author: Andreas Pehrson
patches.subject: Add gtest.

features.summary: Recent updates in the camera and audio capture area of Firefox have introduced a new testing framework that supports **Google Test (gtest)** functionality. This enhancement aims to improve the reliability and stability of features like video capture and audio input, which are essential for applications such as video conferencing and online streaming. By implementing better testing procedures, the developers can catch and fix potential issues before they affect users, leading to a smoother and more dependable experience in browser-based media interactions.

features.label: Objc Video Capture

patches.author: serge-sans-paille
patches.subject: Comply with clang-tidy's modernize-use-nullptr check for dom/

features.summary: Recent updates to the video capture functionality in Firefox have focused on improving code quality by complying with modern coding standards. Specifically, the implementation has adopted the use of `nullptr` instead of traditional null values, following clang-tidy’s guidelines. This change enhances the robustness of the codebase, making it easier to maintain while ensuring compatibility with contemporary C++ development practices. While users may not see immediate visible changes, this type of improvement plays a vital role in maintaining a reliable and effective video capture experience.

features.label: Session negotiation (SDP)

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Migrate Sdp::Clone() (and its concrete-class overrides) to return UniquePtr.

features.summary: Recent updates to the session negotiation component of Firefox focused on improving the handling of Session Description Protocol (SDP) objects, which are crucial for establishing multimedia sessions like voice and video calls. By migrating the `Sdp::Clone()` method to return a `UniquePtr`, Firefox enhances memory management, ensuring more efficient use of resources and reducing potential memory leaks. This change matters because it contributes to a smoother and more reliable user experience during real-time communications, leveraging protocols such as WebRTC — the backbone for modern video conferencing and streaming.

features.label: libwebrtc build

patches.author: Nico Grunbaum
patches.subject: updated default_config_env for v150

features.summary: Recent updates to the libwebrtc build have included changes to the `default_config_env` for version 150, which is part of the foundation for **WebRTC**, the technology that powers real-time audio and video communication in browsers. This adjustment helps streamline the building process and ensures compatibility with the latest enhancements in the WebRTC platform, ultimately aiming to improve performance and reliability in video calls and streaming services. While users may not see an immediate visual change, this work is essential for keeping Firefox equipped with the latest advancements in real-time communication technology.

features.summary: Recent changes in the connection transport area of Firefox have enhanced the WebRTC (Web Real-Time Communication) functionality, which is vital for enabling features like video calls and peer-to-peer communications. Key updates include the implementation of **RTCCertificateStats**, which helps provide insights into the security credentials used during communication, and the introduction of specific metrics such as packets sent and received for candidate pairs, which can improve the performance and quality of these connections. Additionally, updates to **RTCTransportStats** now include information about the TLS version and cipher suites being used, ensuring a more secure and efficient data exchange. These improvements collectively help deliver a better user experience by enhancing the robustness and security of real-time communication on the web.

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC web API, which powers technologies like video calls and real-time communications, include several enhancements to its functionality and performance. Notably, **the implementation of outbound-rtp keyFramesEncoded stats** allows developers to better track video quality by monitoring the number of keyframes encoded, which are crucial for maintaining video clarity during playback. Additionally, the addition of `getRemoteCertificates` enhances security by allowing the retrieval of remote certificates used in sessions, ensuring that communications remain encrypted and secure. Other updates improve the internal handling of statistics and memory management, streamlining how the application allocates resources, which contributes to a more efficient and reliable communication experience.

features.summary: Recent updates to the libwebrtc integration in Firefox have brought important enhancements to video handling, most notably supporting **H.264**, a widely used video compression standard essential for online streaming. The changes streamline the codebase by switching deprecated functions and utilizing more modern memory management techniques, which helps improve the reliability and efficiency of video encoding and decoding processes. Additionally, by refining how the browser assesses its media capabilities and configuring audio streams, these updates ensure better performance and adaptability during video calls, enhancing the overall user experience. _Overall, these improvements contribute to more robust real-time communication features in Firefox._

features.label: Negotiation engine (JSEP)

patches.author: Daniel Holbert

features.summary: Recent updates to the Firefox browser's negotiation engine, which is part of WebRTC (the technology behind video calls), have enhanced its memory management and efficiency through the use of `UniquePtr`, a smart pointer that helps prevent memory leaks. By migrating several cloning methods, including those in JsepCodecDescription and JsepSession, to return `UniquePtr`, the browser can now handle objects more safely and effectively. Additionally, the changes address the treatment of missing RTX (reliable transport) payload types and improve the association of transceivers, which are essential for managing media streams in communication sessions. These optimizations not only bolster performance but also improve the reliability of audio and video connections in real-time interactions, which is **critical for user experience** in video calls and conferencing applications.

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC (the technology behind video calls) functionality in Firefox include the enhancement of its performance metrics and overall reliability. New features like RTCTransportStats, which track the amount of data sent and received during a call, and the improvement of logging practices for better debugging, ensure that developers have the tools they need to monitor and optimize video calls. Furthermore, the implementation of a more robust parser for H.264 video streaming will improve video quality by ensuring that requests and capabilities are better managed. This matters because **_better performance and reliability in video calls can significantly enhance user experience_**, making communications smoother and more efficient.

features.label: Session negotiation (SDP)

features.summary: Recent updates in the Firefox browser focused on improving memory management within session negotiation, specifically around the Session Description Protocol (SDP), which is crucial for setting up and managing communications in technologies like WebRTC (used for video calls). These changes involve using `UniquePtr`, a smart pointer that automatically manages memory, instead of traditional raw pointers, resulting in safer and more efficient code by preventing memory leaks and making ownership clearer. This transition helps maintain the stability and reliability of multimedia features where SDP plays a key role, ensuring a smoother experience when using features like video conferencing or streaming. **Overall, these changes enhance both performance and resilience, especially in communications-heavy applications.**

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC testing framework in Firefox have focused on improving the reliability and coverage of tests for macOS systems. By enabling crashtests, which check how the software behaves under extreme conditions, and re-enabling various mochitests (which ensure that smaller parts of the browser work properly), the team aims to enhance the overall stability of WebRTC, the technology that supports real-time video and audio communication in the browser. These changes are especially relevant for macOS users, ensuring that features like screensharing are robust and function as intended. **This work is crucial because it helps maintain a smooth experience for users engaging in video calls and shared activities online.**

features.label: libwebrtc build

patches.author: Nico Grunbaum

patches.author: Michael Froman
patches.subject: add python script to check for missing branch-head commits.

patches.author: Michael Froman
patches.subject: add python script to show branch-head commits for libwebrtc.

features.summary: Recent updates to the libwebrtc build process included an adjustment to the **default configuration environment to version 150**, which helps ensure developers are using the latest supported features and optimizations for WebRTC, the technology behind video calls. Additionally, two new Python scripts were added: one checks for missing branch-head commits, and the other displays existing branch-head commits for libwebrtc. These scripts enhance the development workflow, making it easier for contributors to keep track of changes and maintain up-to-date builds, thus facilitating smoother collaboration and reducing potential delays in the project’s progress.

features.label: Camera & audio capture

patches.author: Andreas Pehrson
patches.subject: Add gtest.

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 14: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in systemservices.

features.summary: Recent updates in the Camera & audio capture area of Firefox primarily focus on enhancing the logging capabilities for better diagnostics and testing. The introduction of **gtest (Google Test)** will improve the reliability of unit tests, making it easier for developers to ensure that camera and audio features function correctly. Additionally, utilizing **MOZ_LOG_FMT** in system services will streamline how logs are formatted, providing clearer insights into camera and audio performance issues. These changes are crucial for maintaining high standards of functionality and user experience in media capture, ensuring that features like video calls and live streaming work smoothly and efficiently.

features.label: Android camera capture

patches.author: Daniel Baker
patches.subject: Read camera rotation from a window context so it tracks device rotation on Android.

features.summary: Recent updates to the Android camera capture functionality in Firefox now allow the browser to automatically read the camera's rotation based on the device's orientation. This means that when you turn your device, the camera will adjust accordingly, providing a more intuitive experience when taking photos or recording videos. **This improvement enhances user convenience and ensures that images and videos are correctly aligned, mitigating issues with upside-down or sideways captures.**

features.label: Objc Video Capture

patches.author: serge-sans-paille
patches.subject: Comply with clang-tidy's modernize-use-nullptr check for dom/

features.summary: Recent updates to the video capture functionality in Firefox have focused on improving code quality by complying with modern coding standards. Specifically, the implementation has adopted the use of `nullptr` instead of traditional null values, following clang-tidy’s guidelines. This change enhances the robustness of the codebase, making it easier to maintain while ensuring compatibility with contemporary C++ development practices. While users may not see immediate visible changes, this type of improvement plays a vital role in maintaining a reliable and effective video capture experience.

features.label: Test camera capture

patches.author: Byron Campen
patches.subject: Bug 2040478 (MOZ) - Rename GetRealTimeClockRaw, and update a test for a newly-populated stat (was always 0 previously).

features.summary: Recent changes in the camera capture testing area include a renaming of a function to improve clarity and alignment with the latest development standards. Additionally, updates were made to a test related to the real-time clock used in capturing video, which now features a newly populated statistic that was previously returning zero. These adjustments enhance the accuracy and reliability of camera-related functionalities, ensuring better performance for applications utilizing these features, particularly in real-time scenarios.
